heading: (intro)
raw: Directed and written by Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger, based on a fairy tale by Hans Christian Andersen.
clean: Directed and written by Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger, based on a fairy tale by Hans Christian Andersen.

heading: Boris Lermontov
raw: Don't forget, a great impression of simplicity can only be achieved by great agony of body and spirit.
clean: Don't forget, a great impression of simplicity can only be achieved by great agony of body and spirit.

heading: Boris Lermontov
raw: You cannot have it both ways. A dancer who relies upon the doubtful comforts of human love can never be a great dancer. Never.
clean: You cannot have it both ways. A dancer who relies upon the doubtful comforts of human love can never be a great dancer. Never.

heading: Boris Lermontov
raw: [to Julian] It is worth remembering, that it is much more disheartening to have to steal than to be stolen from, hmmm?
clean: It is worth remembering, that it is much more disheartening to have to steal than to be stolen from, hmmm?

heading: Boris Lermontov
raw: [to Vicky] Sorrow will pass, believe me. Life is so unimportant. And from now onwards, you will dance like nobody ever before.
clean: Sorrow will pass, believe me. Life is so unimportant. And from now onwards, you will dance like nobody ever before.

heading: Boris Lermontov
raw: Miss Page is unable to dance tonight, nor indeed any other night.
clean: Miss Page is unable to dance tonight, nor indeed any other night.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Julian: What happens in the end?
clean: What happens in the end?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: Oh, in the end, she dies.
clean: Oh, in the end, she dies.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: Why do you want to dance?
clean: Why do you want to dance?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Vicky: Why do you want to live?
clean: Why do you want to live?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: Well, I don't know exactly why, er, but I must.
clean: Well, I don't know exactly why, er, but I must.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Vicky: That's my answer too.
clean: That's my answer too.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: How would you define ballet, Lady Neston?
clean: How would you define ballet, Lady Neston?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lady Neston: Well, one might call it the poetry of motion perhaps, or...
clean: Well, one might call it the poetry of motion perhaps, or...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: One might. But for me, it is a great deal more. For me, it is a religion. And one doesn't really care to see one's religion practised in an atmosphere... such as this.
clean: One might. But for me, it is a great deal more. For me, it is a religion. And one doesn't really care to see one's religion practised in an atmosphere... such as this.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Vicky Page: To dance.
clean: To dance.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Livy: [just before the curtain raises] You're a magician, Boris. To have produced all this in three weeks, and from nothing.
clean: You're a magician, Boris. To have produced all this in three weeks, and from nothing.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: My dear Livy, not even the best magician in the world can produce a rabbit out of a hat if there is not already a rabbit in the hat.
clean: My dear Livy, not even the best magician in the world can produce a rabbit out of a hat if there is not already a rabbit in the hat.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ljubov: You can't alter human nature.
clean: You can't alter human nature.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lermontov: No? I think you can do even better then that. You can ignore it!
clean: No? I think you can do even better then that. You can ignore it!
